Advanced Text-as-Data – IESP-UERJ Winter School (2025.1, Graduate)
Deep Learning, Transformers, Embeddings, and Large Language Models applied to political science research.
Instructors: Tiago Ventura, Ph.D., and Sebastián Vallejo Vera, Ph.D.
🔗 Course website
Quantitative Methods II – FGV CPDOC (2024.2, Undergraduate)
Exploratory data analysis, basic statistical inference, and regression models.
Instructor: Jairo Nicolau, Ph.D.

Introduction to programming for social sciences.
Instructor: Jimmy Medeiros, Ph.D.

title: 🗃Publications
text:
Open-source tools
💻 mape_municipios_dashboard: a dashboard to interact with one of the most complete databases with information on Brazilian municipalities, collected by MAPE. 2025.
Joint work with Fernando Meireles (IESP-UERJ).
🔗 Source code · 🌐 Access link
💻 RelicárIA: a digital platform that combines AI and data analysis to extract and visualize information from documents. 2025.
Joint work with Danielle Sanches de Almeida (FGV ECMI).
🔗 Source code · 🌐 Access link
💻 BoticárIA: a RAG-based conversational AI that simulates an 18th-century apothecary, providing information on medicinal practices based on historical pharmacological sources from the late 18th century. 2025.
Joint work with Danielle Sanches de Almeida (FGV ECMI).
🔗 Source code · 🌐 Access link

title: Felipe Lamarca
text:
Welcome!👋🏼
I’m a data scientist with a degree from the School of Applied Mathematics (FGV EMAp) and a social scientist with a degree from the School of Social Sciences (FGV CPDOC). Currently, I’m pursuing a Master’s degree in Political Science at the Institute of Social and Political Studies (IESP-UERJ).
I work as a data scientist at AtlasIntel and do research at the Policy and Election Monitoring and Evaluation Laboratory (MAPE).
My research interests include political behavior, public policy, and the application of data science techniques to social science research. This broadly encompasses the use of machine learning, deep learning, causal inference, and survey methods to investigate political phenomena.
📧 felipe dot lamarca at hotmail dot com

title: 👨🏻‍💻 My work
text: AtlasIntel provides advanced data products for political intelligence and economic forecasting. I’m currently working on calibrating survey samples, political analysis, public opinion monitoring, and improving internal data pipelines.
